title: Saturday Gravel Endurance
objective: The week's main aerobic load: a steady Z2 gravel ride that builds durability and keeps weekly volume honest without adding intensity to a high-stress week.
why_now: With Thursday dropped and Friday reduced to tempo, the weekend carries the aerobic volume. Pure endurance on gravel is low-strain, recovery-compatible, and exactly what a stressed system can absorb and benefit from.

source_context.week_objective: Absorb a high-stress week: keep frequency, cut intensity

targets.notes: Z2 by feel, not by the number. Aim for conversational endurance (60-70% FTP, ~155-180 W on the flats); on gravel let terrain move the power around and just keep the effort aerobic. Never let it drift into tempo.

warmup.notes: Roll out easy, let the body and the surface settle before finding endurance rhythm.

steps.notes: Steady aerobic gravel. Keep it conversational - you should be able to talk in full sentences. Climbs will spike power; soft-pedal the descents to even it out. Fuel and drink on a schedule, don't wait until you're empty.

cooldown.notes: Ease the last few km home.

nutrition.pre: Proper breakfast 1.5-3h before, carb-forward; this is the long ride of the week so start topped up.
nutrition.during: 40-60g carbs/hour from the start and drink to thirst; steady fueling is what keeps a long Z2 ride feeling easy the whole way.
nutrition.post: 25-35g protein plus carbs within an hour; a real meal is ideal after the week's main ride.
nutrition.centr_recipe: No new Centr link needed; a normal recovery plate works.

recovery.post_session: Refuel, hydrate, easy walk or legs-up later if stiff; left-calf routine if it tightened on the climbs.
recovery.evening: Keep protecting sleep; a calm Sunday helps you start next week recovered.
recovery.next_morning_check: Sunday is optional - only ride if you genuinely feel like it and wellness is stable; otherwise take the rest.

skill_focus.action: Keep the effort aerobic on the climbs - shift down and spin rather than forcing the power.
skill_focus.why: Letting gravel climbs push you into tempo/threshold turns an easy ride into a hard one and defeats the recovery purpose.

skill_focus.action: Relax on rough surfaces - loose grip, let the bike move under you.
skill_focus.why: Tension on gravel wastes energy and tires the upper body; staying loose keeps a long ride comfortable.

mental_focus.intention: Long, easy, enjoyable. Time on the bike, not numbers.
mental_focus.why: After a stressful week, an unhurried aerobic ride is both the training and the decompression.
mental_focus.reset: If you notice you're pushing, ease back to a pace you could hold for hours and enjoy the terrain.

pre_interval_cues: Dress for showers - it's a cool, wet-ish day.
execution_notes.if_too_easy: Stay easy - the value is the aerobic time, not the watts. You can extend toward 2.5h if you feel good and have the time, but keep it Z2.
execution_notes.if_too_hard: Cut it short to 60-90 minutes or call it - a stressed, under-slept body benefits more from rest than from forcing volume. No guilt.
execution_notes.success_criteria: Finished a steady aerobic ride feeling pleasantly tired, not drained, having stayed conversational throughout.
